steps.title: Find the sampled population
steps.content: The organizer selected $55$ teams at random from all trivia teams in the tournament. A random sample can be generalized back to the population it was selected from, not just to the teams shown in a later table.

steps.title: Separate sample from table
steps.content: The table shows information for only $40$ teams in the sample that practiced at least $3$ hours per week. That table restriction does not change the population from which the original random sample was selected.

steps.title: Use the largest valid group
steps.content: Choice B and Choice C are samples or subsets of the sample, so they are too narrow. Choice A restricts the population by score, but the random selection was not from only teams with that score. The largest population supported by the random selection is all trivia teams in the tournament. The answer is D.
